Marketplace image reference.
Optional. VNet name. If empty, a deterministic name is generated.
Required. VNet CIDR address space (e.g., 192.168.0.0/16).
Optional. Resource ID of an existing DDoS Protection Plan to associate.
Optional. Custom DNS server IP addresses for the VNet.
Required. Subnet definitions for the VNet.
Conditional. Peering configuration to another VNet (hub/spoke). Required if the template establishes hub–spoke peering for this VNet.
Conditional. Peering configuration to a Virtual WAN hub. Required if the template establishes a Virtual WAN hub peering.
Required. Arbitrary key for each tag.
Optional. Tags to apply to the VNet.
Configuration object for the Virtual Network to be deployed.
Required. Resource ID of the peer virtual network.
Conditional. Hub firewall private IP address used for routing (if applicable). Required if the peering must route via a hub firewall.
Optional. Name of the spoke-to-hub peering.
Required. Allow forwarded traffic across the peering.
Required. Allow gateway transit across the peering.
Required. Allow virtual network access across the peering.
Required. Create the reverse peering from hub back to this VNet.
Required. Reverse peering: allow forwarded traffic.
Required. Reverse peering: allow gateway transit.
Required. Reverse peering: allow virtual network access.
Optional. Name of the reverse peering (hub->spoke).
Required. Reverse peering: use remote gateways.
Required. Use remote gateways on the spoke peering.
Peering with another VNet (hub/spoke).
Required. Enables (true) or disables (false) creation/use of this subnet.
Required. Subnet name.
Required. Subnet address prefix in CIDR notation (e.g., 10.0.1.0/24).
Optional. Service delegation for the subnet (resource provider/type).
Optional. Service endpoints to enable for this subnet.
Optional. Resource ID of an associated NAT Gateway.
Conditional. Whether private endpoint network policies are Enabled or Disabled. Required if the subnet will host Private Endpoints and you need policies Disabled.
Conditional. Whether private link service network policies are Enabled or Disabled. Required if the subnet will host a Private Link Service and you need policies Disabled.
Configuration object for one subnet.
Required. Resource ID of the target Virtual WAN hub.
Peering with a Virtual WAN hub.
Required. WAF policy resource name.
Required. WAF policy enabled state.
Required. WAF mode (Prevention or Detection).
Required. Enable request body inspection.
Required. Maximum request body size (KB).
Required. File upload size limit (MB).
Required. WAF policy settings (state, mode, size limits).
Required. Match variable to exclude (e.g., RequestHeaderNames).
Required. Selector value for the match variable.
Required. Selector match operator (e.g., Equals, Contains).
Required. Rule set type (e.g., OWASP).
Required. Rule set version (e.g., 3.2).
Optional. Rule groups to exclude.
Optional. Specific managed rule set exclusion details.
Optional. Exclusions for specific rules or variables.
Required. Rule set type (e.g., OWASP).
Required. Rule set version.
Required. Name of the rule group.
Required. Rule ID.
Required. Action to take (e.g., Allow, Block, Log).
Required. Whether the rule is enabled.
Required. Rule overrides within the group.
Optional. Overrides for specific rule groups.
Required. Managed rule sets to apply.
Required. Managed rule sets and exclusions for the WAF policy.
Required. Arbitrary key for each tag.
Optional. Tags to apply to the WAF Policy.
Configuration object for the Web Application Firewall (WAF) Policy to be deployed.
Optional. Specify the name of lock.
Optional. Specify the type of lock.
Optional. Specify the notes of the lock.
An AVM-aligned type for a lock.
Required. APIM resource name (usually your varApimName).
Required. Azure region for the primary location.
